cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling

threat.title: DynamoDB Throttling Attack via Hot Partition and Scan Operation Abuse
threat.description: Attackers exploit DynamoDB's partition-based architecture to cause service degradation through hot partition attacks, scan operation abuse, and read/write capacity exhaustion. By targeting predictable partition keys or triggering expensive full-table scans, adversaries can exhaust provisioned throughput, trigger cascading failures across dependent services (Lambda, API Gateway), and cause widespread application denial of service. This is particularly severe in serverless architectures where DynamoDB throttling creates retry storms that amplify the attack impact.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies DynamoDB table with predictable partition keys (e.g., date-based, sequential IDs) through API enumeration
2. Crafts malicious requests targeting a single hot partition key repeatedly (e.g., today's date for time-series data)
3. Sends concurrent API requests exceeding partition's 3000 RCU or 1000 WCU per-second limit
4. DynamoDB begins throttling with ProvisionedThroughputExceededException errors
5. Triggers retry storms from application layer (Lambda, microservices) amplifying the load by 10-50x
6. Cascading failure spreads to dependent services as DynamoDB throttling causes Lambda timeouts, SQS delays, and API Gateway 5xx errors

Alternative attack vector: Attacker exploits unauthenticated or poorly-protected scan operations via public API endpoints, causing full-table scans that consume massive read capacity (charged by data scanned, not returned). A single 100GB table scan can cost $12.50 and consume 20,000+ RCUs, exhausting capacity for legitimate traffic.
prerequisites: DynamoDB table with provisioned capacity mode OR on-demand mode without AWS WAF rate limiting
Predictable or low-cardinality partition keys (dates, status codes, single tenant ID)
Public or poorly authenticated API endpoints exposing DynamoDB scan/query operations
Missing application-level throttling or request validation
No CloudWatch alarms for throttle events or capacity exhaustion

cve_references.description: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption - Database queries that take a long time to process are good DoS targets

real_world_breaches.impact: 24-hour cascading failure affecting 142 AWS services including Lambda, EC2, SQS, and IAM across us-east-1 region. DynamoDB DNS failure triggered retry storms that overwhelmed internal resolvers.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: DynamoDB DNS race condition created empty endpoint record, causing cascading throttling across all dependent AWS services. IAM stored policies in DynamoDB, amplifying the blast radius to all services requiring authentication.

real_world_breaches.impact: Production pipeline paralyzed for hours due to DynamoDB throttling causing exponential Lambda retry amplification. Each throttled request generated 5-10 retries across service layers, multiplying costs and latency.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: DynamoDB hot partition throttling triggered non-idempotent Lambda retries that compounded stress instead of dissipating it, creating excess costs and duplicate processing.

real_world_breaches.incident: DynamoDB Single Hot Partition Table Failure
real_world_breaches.impact: Entire production DynamoDB table taken offline by a single hot partition receiving concentrated traffic, despite adequate overall table capacity.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Poor partition key design caused all writes to concentrate on one partition key value, exceeding the per-partition throughput limit of 1000 WCU/second.

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Unauthenticated API endpoint allowing full-table DynamoDB scans causing $500/hour cost spike and service degradation

security_research.title: Enhanced Throttling Observability in Amazon DynamoDB
security_research.key_finding: AWS introduced 8 new CloudWatch metrics in August 2025 for granular throttling detection, enabling identification of exact partition keys, indexes, and throttle reasons down to individual operations

security_research.title: DynamoDB Scan - Why You Should (Almost) Never Use It
security_research.key_finding: DynamoDB scan cost depends on data scanned, not returned. Full table scans have O(n) complexity and can cost 10-100x more than queries, making them prime DoS attack vectors

security_research.title: 12 Amazon DynamoDB Security Best Practices
security_research.key_finding: Use Auto Scaling and API throttling limits to prevent DoS attacks. Set CloudWatch alarms for ProvisionedThroughputExceeded and implement VPC endpoint policies to limit scan operations

mitigations.title: Enable DynamoDB Auto Scaling with Exponential Backoff Retry Logic
mitigations.description: Implement DynamoDB Auto Scaling to automatically adjust read/write capacity during traffic spikes, combined with AWS SDK exponential backoff to gracefully handle throttling events and prevent retry storms. This prevents both accidental capacity exhaustion and malicious DoS attacks.
iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.DS-4 - Adequate capacity to ensure availability
DE.AE-5 - Incident alert thresholds are established
validation_steps: Enable Auto Scaling for both read and write capacity with 70% target utilization
Configure exponential backoff in AWS SDK with minimum 10 retry attempts and jitter
Test throttling behavior with load testing tool (Artillery, Locust) ramping to 2x capacity
Verify Auto Scaling triggers within 60 seconds of capacity breach
Implement circuit breaker pattern to prevent cascading Lambda failures

mitigations.title: Design High-Cardinality Partition Keys and Disable Scan Operations
mitigations.description: Prevent hot partition attacks by designing partition keys with high cardinality that distribute load evenly across partitions (e.g., hashed user IDs, UUIDs). Explicitly deny scan operations via IAM policies and VPC endpoint policies unless absolutely necessary, forcing attackers to use more expensive query operations.
nist_csf: PR.AC-4 - Access permissions and authorizations are managed
validation_steps: Analyze partition key cardinality using CloudWatch Contributor Insights (target: >10,000 unique keys)
Test IAM policy with aws dynamodb scan command (should return AccessDeniedException)
Verify VPC endpoint policy blocks scan operations from EC2 instances
Load test with 1000 RPS across different partition keys (monitor ProvisionedThroughputExceeded metric should be zero)
Use NoSQL Workbench to visualize access patterns and identify hot partitions

mitigations.title: Implement AWS WAF Rate Limiting and API Gateway Request Validation
mitigations.description: Deploy AWS WAF in front of API Gateway to enforce rate limiting (e.g., 100 requests/5 minutes per IP) and block suspicious traffic patterns before they reach DynamoDB. Combine with API Gateway request validation to reject malformed queries and limit scan operations.

code_example.code:
# CloudFormation template for WAF + API Gateway protection
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'WAF Rate Limiting for DynamoDB API Protection'

Resources:
  # WAF Web ACL with rate limiting
  DynamoDBAPIWebACL:
    Type: AWS::WAFv2::WebACL
    Properties:
      Name: DynamoDBAPIProtection
      Scope: REGIONAL
      DefaultAction:
        Allow: {}
      Rules:
        # Rule 1: Rate limit per IP (prevent burst attacks)
        - Name: RateLimitPerIP
          Priority: 1
          Statement:
            RateBasedStatement:
              Limit: 100 # Max 100 requests per 5 minutes
              AggregateKeyType: IP
          Action:
            Block:
              CustomResponse:
                ResponseCode: 429
                CustomResponseBodyKey: TooManyRequests
          VisibilityConfig:
            SampledRequestsEnabled: true
            CloudWatchMetricsEnabled: true
            MetricName: RateLimitPerIP
        
        # Rule 2: Block scan operations in query strings
        - Name: BlockScanOperations
          Priority: 2
          Statement:
            OrStatement:
              Statements:
                - ByteMatchStatement:
                    SearchString: 'scan'
                    FieldToMatch:
                      UriPath: {}
                    TextTransformations:
                      - Priority: 0
                        Type: LOWERCASE
                - ByteMatchStatement:
                    SearchString: 'TableName'
                    FieldToMatch:
                      Body:
                        OversizeHandling: CONTINUE
                    TextTransformations:
                      - Priority: 0
                        Type: NONE
          Action:
            Block:
              CustomResponse:
                ResponseCode: 403
          VisibilityConfig:
            SampledRequestsEnabled: true
            CloudWatchMetricsEnabled: true
            MetricName: BlockedScanOperations
        
        # Rule 3: AWS Managed Rule - Core Rule Set (SQL injection, XSS)
        - Name: AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet
          Priority: 3
          OverrideAction:
            None: {}
          Statement:
            ManagedRuleGroupStatement:
              VendorName: AWS
              Name: AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet
          VisibilityConfig:
            SampledRequestsEnabled: true
            CloudWatchMetricsEnabled: true
            MetricName: AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet
      
      VisibilityConfig:
        SampledRequestsEnabled: true
        CloudWatchMetricsEnabled: true
        MetricName: DynamoDBAPIWebACL
      
      CustomResponseBodies:
        TooManyRequests:
          ContentType: APPLICATION_JSON
          Content: '{"error": "Rate limit exceeded. Please retry after 5 minutes."}'

  # API Gateway with request validation
  DynamoDBAPIGateway:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
    Properties:
      Name: DynamoDBProtectedAPI
      Description: API Gateway with WAF protection and request validation
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Types:
          - REGIONAL
  
  # Request validator for strict input validation
  RequestValidator:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RequestValidator
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref DynamoDBAPIGateway
      ValidateRequestBody: true
      ValidateRequestParameters: true
      Name: RequestBodyAndParametersValidator
  
  # API Gateway model for request validation
  QueryRequestModel:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Model
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref DynamoDBAPIGateway
      ContentType: application/json
      Name: QueryRequestModel
      Schema:
        $schema: 'http://json-schema.org/draft-04/schema#'
        type: object
        properties:
          userId:
            type: string
            pattern: '^[a-zA-Z0-9-]{1,100}$'
          limit:
            type: integer
            minimum: 1
            maximum: 100
          lastEvaluatedKey:
            type: object
        required:
          - userId
        additionalProperties: false
  
  # Associate WAF with API Gateway
  WAFAssociation:
    Type: AWS::WAFv2::WebACLAssociation
    Properties:
      ResourceArn: !Sub 'arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}::/restapis/${DynamoDBAPIGateway}/stages/prod'
      WebACLArn: !GetAtt DynamoDBAPIWebACL.Arn
  
  # CloudWatch Alarm for rate limit triggers
  RateLimitAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: DynamoDB-WAF-RateLimit-Triggered
      AlarmDescription: Alert when WAF rate limiting blocks excessive requests
      MetricName: BlockedRequests
      Namespace: AWS/WAFV2
      Statistic: Sum
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 10
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: Rule
          Value: RateLimitPerIP
        - Name: WebACL
          Value: !GetAtt DynamoDBAPIWebACL.Name
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref SecurityAlertSNSTopic
  
  SecurityAlertSNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: DynamoDB-Security-Alerts
      DisplayName: DynamoDB API Security Alerts

Outputs:
  WebACLArn:
    Value: !GetAtt DynamoDBAPIWebACL.Arn
  APIGatewayId:
    Value: !Ref DynamoDBAPIGateway

tools.name: AWS WAF
tools.cost: $5/month per Web ACL + $1 per million requests

iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Deploy WAF Web ACL and associate with API Gateway stage
Test rate limiting with curl/Apache Bench exceeding 100 req/5min (should return HTTP 429)
Verify request validation rejects queries without required userId parameter
Configure CloudWatch Alarms for BlockedRequests metric with SNS notifications
Enable WAF logging to S3 for forensic analysis of blocked requests

mitigations.title: Deploy CloudWatch Contributor Insights and Throttle-Specific Alarms
mitigations.description: Enable the 8 new DynamoDB throttle metrics introduced in August 2025 to detect hot partitions, GSI throttling, and account limit breaches in real-time. Use CloudWatch Contributor Insights to identify the exact partition keys causing throttling and trigger automated remediation.

code_example.code:
# CloudFormation: CloudWatch Alarms for DynamoDB throttling detection
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  # Alarm for Write Throttling Events
  WriteThrottleAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: DynamoDB-WriteThrottle-Orders-Table
      AlarmDescription: Triggers when write operations are throttled
      MetricName: WriteThrottleEvents
      Namespace: AWS/DynamoDB
      Statistic: Sum
      Period: 60
      EvaluationPeriods: 2
      Threshold: 10
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: TableName
          Value: Orders
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref ThrottleAlertTopic
      TreatMissingData: notBreaching
  
  # Alarm for Read Throttling Events
  ReadThrottleAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: DynamoDB-ReadThrottle-Orders-Table
      AlarmDescription: Triggers when read operations are throttled
      MetricName: ReadThrottleEvents
      Namespace: AWS/DynamoDB
      Statistic: Sum
      Period: 60
      EvaluationPeriods: 2
      Threshold: 10
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: TableName
          Value: Orders
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref ThrottleAlertTopic
  
  # Alarm for GSI Write Throttling (back-pressure)
  GSIWriteThrottleAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: DynamoDB-GSI-WriteThrottle-Orders-GSI1
      AlarmDescription: Triggers when GSI write capacity is exhausted causing base table throttling
      MetricName: WriteThrottleEvents
      Namespace: AWS/DynamoDB
      Statistic: Sum
      Period: 60
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 5
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: TableName
          Value: Orders
        - Name: GlobalSecondaryIndexName
          Value: GSI1
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref ThrottleAlertTopic
  
  # Alarm for Account-Level Throttling
  AccountLimitThrottleAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: DynamoDB-AccountLimit-Throttle
      AlarmDescription: Triggers when account-level read/write limits are exceeded
      MetricName: AccountProvisionedReadCapacityUtilization
      Namespace: AWS/DynamoDB
      Statistic: Average
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 80
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref ThrottleAlertTopic
  
  ThrottleAlertTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: DynamoDB-Throttle-Alerts
      Subscription:
        - Endpoint: security-team@example.com
          Protocol: email
  
  # Contributor Insights Rule to identify hot partition keys
  ContributorInsightsRule:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: Orders
      ContributorInsightsSpecification:
        Enabled: true

# Enable via AWS CLI for existing tables:
# aws dynamodb update-contributor-insights \
#   --table-name Orders \
#   --contributor-insights-action ENABLE

# Query Contributor Insights to find hot keys:
# aws cloudwatch get-metric-data \
#   --metric-data-queries file://contributor-insights-query.json \
#   --start-time 2026-01-30T00:00:00Z \
#   --end-time 2026-01-30T23:59:59Z

# contributor-insights-query.json:
# {
#   "Id": "throttledKeys",
#   "MetricStat": {
#     "Metric": {
#       "Namespace": "AWS/DynamoDB/Contributor-Insights",
#       "MetricName": "ThrottledRequestsPerPartitionKey",
#       "Dimensions": [
#         {"Name": "TableName", "Value": "Orders"}
#       ]
#     },
#     "Period": 300,
#     "Stat": "Maximum"
#   }
# }

# CloudWatch Logs Insights Query for DynamoDB Throttling Analysis
# Run in CloudWatch Logs Insights console:

fields @timestamp, @message, userIdentity.principalId, requestParameters.tableName, errorCode, errorMessage
| filter eventSource = "dynamodb.amazonaws.com"
| filter errorCode in ["ProvisionedThroughputExceededException", "RequestLimitExceeded", "ThrottlingException"]
| stats count() as ThrottleCount by requestParameters.tableName, userIdentity.principalId, errorCode
| sort ThrottleCount desc
| limit 20

# Alternative: Use new throttle-reason-specific metrics (August 2025+)
# Metrics now include:
# - ReadThrottleEvents (with dimension: ThrottleReason=ProvisionedThroughputExceeded)
# - WriteThrottleEvents (with dimension: ThrottleReason=KeyRangeThroughputExceeded)
# - ReadThrottleEvents (with dimension: ThrottleReason=AccountLimitExceeded)
# - WriteThrottleEvents (with dimension: ThrottleReason=IndexWriteProvisionedThroughputExceeded)

tools.name: CloudWatch Contributor Insights
tools.cost: $0.50 per million log events + $0.50 per rule/month

nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated
validation_steps: Enable Contributor Insights on all production DynamoDB tables
Create CloudWatch Alarms for WriteThrottleEvents and ReadThrottleEvents (threshold: 10 events/min)
Test alarm triggers with simulated hot partition load (Artillery script)
Configure SNS topic with email/Slack notifications for throttle events
Run CloudWatch Logs Insights query to identify top throttled tables and IAM principals

indicators: CloudWatch metric WriteThrottleEvents or ReadThrottleEvents sustained above baseline
Contributor Insights showing 80%+ requests to a single partition key
Lambda function concurrent execution limit reached due to DynamoDB retries
Unusual API Gateway 5xx error rate correlated with DynamoDB throttling
High ConsumedReadCapacityUnits on table with low data return (indicates scan abuse)
GSI WriteThrottleEvents causing back-pressure on base table writes
Account-level throttling (RequestLimitExceeded) despite table-level capacity available

log_queries.query:
// Detect DynamoDB throttling attacks via CloudTrail logs
AWSCloudTrail
| where EventSource == "dynamodb.amazonaws.com"
| where ErrorCode in ("ProvisionedThroughputExceededException", "RequestLimitExceeded", "ThrottlingException")
| extend TableName = tostring(RequestParameters.tableName)
| extend SourceIP = tostring(SourceIpAddress)
| summarize ThrottleCount = count(), UniqueIPs = dcount(SourceIP) by TableName, UserIdentityPrincipalId, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where ThrottleCount > 50 // More than 50 throttle events in 5 minutes
| order by ThrottleCount desc
| project TimeGenerated, TableName, UserIdentityPrincipalId, ThrottleCount, UniqueIPs

index=aws_cloudtrail sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail eventSource=dynamodb.amazonaws.com
  (errorCode="ProvisionedThroughputExceededException" OR errorCode="RequestLimitExceeded" OR errorCode="ThrottlingException")
| eval table_name=spath(_raw, "requestParameters.tableName")
| eval source_ip=spath(_raw, "sourceIPAddress")
| eval principal=spath(_raw, "userIdentity.principalId")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as throttle_count dc(source_ip) as unique_ips by _time, table_name, principal
| where throttle_count > 50
| sort -throttle_count
| table _time, table_name, principal, throttle_count, unique_ips

event.provider: "dynamodb.amazonaws.com" AND event.outcome: "failure" AND (aws.cloudtrail.error_code: "ProvisionedThroughputExceededException" OR aws.cloudtrail.error_code: "RequestLimitExceeded")
| stats count() by aws.cloudtrail.request_parameters.tableName, user.id, date_histogram(@timestamp, "5m")
| where count > 50

# Query for DynamoDB throttle events with partition key analysis
fields @timestamp, requestParameters.tableName, requestParameters.key, errorCode, errorMessage, sourceIPAddress, userIdentity.principalId
| filter eventSource = "dynamodb.amazonaws.com"
| filter errorCode in ["ProvisionedThroughputExceededException", "ThrottlingException", "RequestLimitExceeded"]
| parse requestParameters.key /"(?<partitionKey>[^,]+)/
| stats count() as throttleEvents by requestParameters.tableName, partitionKey, sourceIPAddress, bin(5m)
| filter throttleEvents > 20
| sort throttleEvents desc
| limit 50

log_queries.siem: AWS CloudWatch Logs Insights - Scan Abuse Detection
log_queries.query:
# Detect expensive scan operations causing DoS
fields @timestamp, requestParameters.tableName, requestParameters.scanFilter, responseElements.scannedCount, responseElements.count, billedSizeBytes
| filter eventName = "Scan"
| filter responseElements.scannedCount > 100000 OR billedSizeBytes > 10485760
| stats sum(responseElements.scannedCount) as totalScanned, sum(billedSizeBytes) as totalBilled, count() as scanCount by requestParameters.tableName, userIdentity.principalId, bin(1h)
| filter scanCount > 10 or totalScanned > 1000000
| sort totalBilled desc

tools.name: AWS X-Ray
tools.type: Distributed Tracing for Throttle Root Cause
tools.usage: Trace Lambda->DynamoDB calls to identify retry amplification patterns

tools.name: DynamoDB Export to S3 + Athena
tools.usage: Analyze partition key distribution across full table dataset to identify skew
